package repository

import (
	"net/http"

	"github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
)

// Positive scenario for response_dto_outside_transport_package.
// Anti-pattern: Response DTO is defined in the repository package
// instead of the transport/handler package, violating layer boundaries.

// Anti-pattern: This response DTO lives in the repository package but is
// serialized directly into HTTP responses.
type InvoiceResponse struct {
	ID         uint    `json:"id"`
	OrderID    uint    `json:"order_id"`
	Amount     float64 `json:"amount"`
	DueDate    string  `json:"due_date"`
	Currency   string  `json:"currency"`
	Status     string  `json:"status"`
}

type InvoiceRepository struct {
	db *gorm.DB
}

func (r *InvoiceRepository) GetInvoice(c *gin.Context) {
	var invoice InvoiceResponse
	if err := r.db.Where("id = ?", c.Param("id")).First(&invoice).Error; err != nil {
		c.JSON(http.StatusNotFound, gin.H{"error": "invoice not found"})
		return
	}

	// Anti-pattern: repository defines and returns the HTTP response DTO
	c.JSON(http.StatusOK, invoice)
}
